stem: The limaçon \( r=[[a]]+[[b]]\cos\theta \) is graphed in polar coordinates. Find \( r \) at \( \theta=\dfrac{[[k]]\pi}{[[n]]} \).
distractor_rationales: This subtracts the cosine term instead of adding it.
This multiplies \( a\) and \( b\) instead of adding \( a\) to \( b\cos\theta\).
This swaps the roles of \( a\) and \( b\).
This is off by one from the correct value of \( r\).
This is off by one (other direction) from the correct value of \( r\).

stem: The rose curve \( r=[[a]]\cos([[pet]]\theta) \) is graphed in polar coordinates. Find \( r \) at \( \theta=\dfrac{[[k]]\pi}{[[n]]} \).
distractor_rationales: This has the wrong sign for this angle.
This forgets to multiply the angle by the petal count \( [[pet]]\) before taking the cosine.
This multiplies the petal count by the amplitude \( a\) instead of by the angle.
This is off by one from the correct value of \( r\).
This is off by one (other direction) from the correct value of \( r\).

stem: The Archimedean spiral \( r=[[a]]\theta \) is graphed in polar coordinates for \( \theta\ge 0 \). Find \( r \) at \( \theta=\dfrac{[[k]]\pi}{[[n]]} \).
distractor_rationales: This adds \( a\) to \( \theta\) instead of multiplying them.
This inverts the relationship, dividing \( a\) and \( n\) by \( k\) instead of scaling \( \theta\) by \( a\).
This inverts the whole expression rather than multiplying \( a\) by \( \theta\).
This drops the factor of \( \pi\) from the angle entirely.
This is off by one from the correct value of \( r\).
This is off by one (other direction) from the correct value of \( r\).
